parsed_artifacts.content:
top_module:
  name: timer
  file: rtl/timer.sv
  version: '1.0'
  type: peripheral
  description: Small APB-style programmable down-counter timer with auto-reload and single-cycle interrupt pulse.
  reference_spec: timer/req/timer_requirements.md
  target:
    technology: generic
    clock_freq_mhz: 100
    area_um2: null
    power_mw: null

sub_modules:
  - name: timer_regs
    file: rtl/timer_regs.sv
    ownership: manifest
    ssot_gen: true
    implements:
      - registers.register_list
      - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_LOAD
      - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_CTRL
      - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_READ_STATUS
      - error_handling
    source_sections:
      - registers
      - function_model
      - error_handling
    register_refs:
      - registers.register_list.LOAD
      - registers.register_list.CTRL
      - registers.register_list.STATUS
    function_model_refs:
      - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_LOAD
      - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_CTRL
      - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_READ_STATUS
    connections:
      - { module: timer_regs, port: clk, signal: pclk }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: resetn, signal: presetn }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: paddr, signal: paddr }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: psel, signal: psel }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: penable, signal: penable }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: pwrite, signal: pwrite }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: pwdata, signal: pwdata }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: prdata, signal: prdata }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: pready, signal: pready }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: pslverr, signal: pslverr }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: load_q, signal: load_q }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: enable_q, signal: enable_q }
      - { module: timer_regs, port: count_q, signal: count_q }
    description: APB register decode and readback block for LOAD, CTRL, and STATUS.
  - name: timer_core
    file: rtl/timer_core.sv
    ownership: manifest
    ssot_gen: false
    implements:
      - function_model.state_variables.timer_count_q
      - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DISABLED
      - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DECREMENT
      - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_EXPIRE_RELOAD
      - cycle_model.pipeline
      - cycle_model.handshake_rules
      - interrupts.sources.IRQ_PULSE
    source_sections:
      - function_model
      - cycle_model
      - interrupts
    function_model_refs:
      - function_model.state_variables
      - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DISABLED
      - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DECREMENT
      - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_EXPIRE_RELOAD
    cycle_model_refs:
      - cycle_model.pipeline
      - cycle_model.handshake_rules
    fsm_refs:
      - fsm.timer_control
    connections:
      - { module: timer_core, port: clk, signal: pclk }
      - { module: timer_core, port: resetn, signal: presetn }
      - { module: timer_core, port: load_i, signal: load_q }
      - { module: timer_core, port: enable_i, signal: enable_q }
      - { module: timer_core, port: count_o, signal: count_q }
      - { module: timer_core, port: irq_o, signal: irq }
    description: Timer decrement, zero-detect, auto-reload, and IRQ pulse behavior.

decomposition:
  units:
    - id: apb_registers
      kind: control/status
      source_refs:
        - registers.register_list
        - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_LOAD
        - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_CTRL
        - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_READ_STATUS
      rtl_candidates:
        - timer_regs
      verification_impact:
        - test_requirements.scenarios.SC_APB_PROGRAM_LOAD_ENABLE
        - test_requirements.scenarios.SC_APB_READ_STATUS
    - id: countdown_engine
      kind: sequential_datapath
      source_refs:
        - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DISABLED
        - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DECREMENT
        - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_EXPIRE_RELOAD
        - cycle_model.pipeline
      rtl_candidates:
        - timer_core
      verification_impact:
        - test_requirements.scenarios.SC_COUNTDOWN_IRQ_RELOAD
        - test_requirements.scenarios.SC_DISABLE_HOLDS

parameters:
  - name: DATA_WIDTH
    default: 32
    type: int
    description: Width of LOAD, STATUS count, and APB data path.
    drives:
      - timer_regs.sv
      - timer_core.sv
  - name: ADDR_WIDTH
    default: 8
    type: int
    description: APB byte address width for the small timer register map.
    drives:
      - timer_regs.sv
  - name: RESET_POLARITY
    default: active_low
    type: enum
    values:
      - active_low
      - active_high
    description: Main reset polarity; timer uses active-low presetn.
    drives:
      - timer.sv
      - timer_regs.sv
      - timer_core.sv

io_list:
  clock_domains:
    - name: pclk_domain
      frequency_mhz: 100
      description: APB and timer counter clock domain.
      ports:
        - name: pclk
          width: 1
          direction: input
          description: APB/timer clock.
  resets:
    - name: presetn
      polarity: active_low
      sync_async: async_assert_sync_deassert
      description: Active-low reset; after deassertion timer count is zero and disabled.
      ports:
        - name: presetn
          width: 1
          direction: input
          description: Active-low reset.
  interfaces:
    - name: apb_slave
      type: APB-style
      role: slave
      description: Simple APB-style register access interface.
      ports:
        - { name: paddr, width: 8, direction: input, description: Byte address for timer registers. }
        - { name: psel, width: 1, direction: input, description: APB select. }
        - { name: penable, width: 1, direction: input, description: APB enable phase. }
        - { name: pwrite, width: 1, direction: input, description: APB write control. }
        - { name: pwdata, width: 32, direction: input, description: APB write data. }
        - { name: prdata, width: 32, direction: output, description: APB read data. }
        - { name: pready, width: 1, direction: output, description: APB transfer ready; single-cycle response. }
        - { name: pslverr, width: 1, direction: output, description: APB error response for invalid addresses. }
    - name: interrupt
      type: custom
      role: output
      description: Single-cycle interrupt pulse generated when enabled counter reaches zero.
      ports:
        - { name: irq, width: 1, direction: output, description: Active-high one-clock pulse on timer expiry. }

features:
  - name: APB programmable load
    trigger: Software writes 32-bit LOAD register.
    datapath: pwdata is captured into load_q when APB write to LOAD is accepted.
    control: APB write acceptance in timer_regs.
    output: New load_q value used for subsequent reloads.
  - name: Enable-controlled countdown
    trigger: Software writes CTRL.ENABLE to one.
    datapath: timer_count_q decrements by one each pclk cycle while enable_q is one and count is non-zero.
    control: timer_core enabled counting state.
    output: STATUS read exposes current timer_count_q.
  - name: Auto-reload and irq pulse
    trigger: Enabled timer observes timer_count_q equal to zero at the tick boundary.
    datapath: timer_count_q is reloaded from load_q and irq is asserted for exactly one cycle.
    control: timer_core expiry path.
    output: irq single-cycle active-high pulse and continued counting from LOAD value.
  - name: Disable holds counter
    trigger: Software writes CTRL.ENABLE to zero.
    datapath: timer_count_q holds its current value while disabled.
    control: timer_core disabled hold path.
    output: STATUS remains stable except for APB read muxing.

dataflow:
  register_write_path:
    source: apb_slave.pwdata
    destination: LOAD.load_value or CTRL.enable
    sequence: APB setup then enable phase accepted with psel==1 and penable==1 and pwrite==1.
  register_read_path:
    source: STATUS.count field or other readable registers
    destination: apb_slave.prdata
    sequence: APB read accepted with psel==1 and penable==1 and pwrite==0.
  countdown_path:
    source: timer_count_q and load_q
    destination: timer_count_q and irq
    sequence: If enabled and count is non-zero, decrement; if enabled and count is zero, pulse irq and reload LOAD.

function_model:
  purpose: Executable behavior source for cocotb/pyuvm scoreboard and FL-vs-RTL comparison of the timer peripheral.
  state_variables:
    - name: load_q
      source: registers.LOAD.load_value
      width: 32
      reset: 0
      description: Programmed reload value.
    - name: enable_q
      source: registers.CTRL.enable
      width: 1
      reset: 0
      description: Counter enable control bit.
    - name: timer_count_q
      source: registers.STATUS.count
      width: 32
      reset: 0
      description: Current down-counter value exposed by STATUS.
    - name: irq_q
      width: 1
      reset: 0
      description: One-cycle interrupt pulse state.
  transactions:
    - id: FM_APB_WRITE_LOAD
      name: apb_write_load
      preconditions:
        - psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and paddr == 0
      inputs:
        - pwdata
      outputs:
        - pready == 1
        - pslverr == 0
        - irq_q == 0
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_next, port: pready, expr: '1', width: 1 }
        - { name: pslverr_next, port: pslverr, expr: '0', width: 1 }
        - { name: irq_next, port: irq, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      state_updates:
        - { name: load_q, expr: 'pwdata & 0xFFFFFFFF', width: 32 }
        - { name: irq_q, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - load_q is updated with pwdata[31:0].
        - irq_q is cleared during register write handling.
      error_cases: []
      sample_stage: APB_ACCESS
    - id: FM_APB_WRITE_CTRL
      name: apb_write_ctrl
      preconditions:
        - psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and paddr == 4
      inputs:
        - pwdata
      outputs:
        - pready == 1
        - pslverr == 0
        - irq_q == 0
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_next, port: pready, expr: '1', width: 1 }
        - { name: pslverr_next, port: pslverr, expr: '0', width: 1 }
        - { name: irq_next, port: irq, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      state_updates:
        - { name: enable_q, expr: 'pwdata & 1', width: 1 }
        - { name: irq_q, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - enable_q is updated from pwdata bit 0; writing zero disables and holds the counter.
        - irq_q is cleared during register write handling.
      error_cases: []
      sample_stage: APB_ACCESS
    - id: FM_APB_READ_STATUS
      name: apb_read_status
      preconditions:
        - psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 0 and paddr == 8
      inputs: []
      outputs:
        - pready == 1
        - pslverr == 0
        - prdata == timer_count_q
        - irq_q == 0
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_next, port: pready, expr: '1', width: 1 }
        - { name: pslverr_next, port: pslverr, expr: '0', width: 1 }
        - { name: prdata_next, port: prdata, expr: 'timer_count_q & 0xFFFFFFFF', width: 32 }
        - { name: irq_next, port: irq, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      state_updates:
        - { name: irq_q, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - STATUS read returns current timer_count_q without changing the count.
        - irq_q is cleared unless a timer expiry transaction occurs in the timer tick path.
      error_cases: []
      sample_stage: APB_ACCESS
    - id: FM_APB_INVALID
      name: apb_invalid_access
      preconditions:
        - psel == 1 and penable == 1 and ((paddr != 0 and paddr != 4 and paddr != 8) or (pwrite == 1 and paddr == 8))
      inputs:
        - paddr
        - pwrite
      outputs:
        - pready == 1
        - pslverr == 1
        - irq_q == 0
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_next, port: pready, expr: '1', width: 1 }
        - { name: pslverr_next, port: pslverr, expr: '1', width: 1 }
        - { name: irq_next, port: irq, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      state_updates:
        - { name: irq_q, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - Invalid register addresses and writes to read-only STATUS report pslverr and do not update architectural timer state.
      error_cases:
        - condition: (paddr != 0 and paddr != 4 and paddr != 8) or (pwrite == 1 and paddr == 8)
          result: pslverr is asserted for the accepted APB transfer.
      sample_stage: APB_ACCESS
    - id: FM_TICK_DISABLED
      name: tick_disabled_hold
      preconditions:
        - psel == 0 and enable_q == 0
      inputs: []
      outputs:
        - irq_q == 0
      output_rules:
        - { name: irq_next, port: irq, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      state_updates:
        - { name: timer_count_q, expr: 'timer_count_q & 0xFFFFFFFF', width: 32 }
        - { name: irq_q, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - timer_count_q holds its current value while disabled.
        - irq_q remains deasserted.
      error_cases: []
      sample_stage: TICK_COMMIT
    - id: FM_TICK_DECREMENT
      name: tick_enabled_decrement
      preconditions:
        - psel == 0 and enable_q == 1 and timer_count_q > 0
      inputs: []
      outputs:
        - irq_q == 0
      output_rules:
        - { name: irq_next, port: irq, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      state_updates:
        - { name: timer_count_q, expr: '(timer_count_q - 1) & 0xFFFFFFFF', width: 32 }
        - { name: irq_q, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - timer_count_q decrements by one on the clock edge.
        - irq_q remains deasserted.
      error_cases: []
      sample_stage: TICK_COMMIT
    - id: FM_TICK_EXPIRE_RELOAD
      name: tick_expire_reload_irq
      preconditions:
        - psel == 0 and enable_q == 1 and timer_count_q == 0
      inputs: []
      outputs:
        - irq_q == 1
      output_rules:
        - { name: irq_next, port: irq, expr: '1', width: 1 }
      state_updates:
        - { name: timer_count_q, expr: 'load_q & 0xFFFFFFFF', width: 32 }
        - { name: irq_q, expr: '1', width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - irq_q asserts for exactly the expiry clock cycle.
        - timer_count_q reloads from load_q and counting continues while enable_q remains one.
      error_cases: []
      sample_stage: TICK_COMMIT
    - id: FM_IDLE
      name: idle_no_transfer
      preconditions: []
      inputs: []
      outputs:
        - pready == 0
        - pslverr == 0
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_next, port: pready, expr: '0', width: 1 }
        - { name: pslverr_next, port: pslverr, expr: '0', width: 1 }
      state_updates: []
      side_effects:
        - No architectural state changes.
      error_cases: []
      sample_stage: IDLE
  invariants:
    - reset implies load_q == 0 and enable_q == 0 and timer_count_q == 0 and irq_q == 0
    - irq_q == 1 implies enable_q == 1 and timer_count_q == load_q
    - enable_q == 0 implies irq_q == 0
    - STATUS.count mirrors timer_count_q[31:0]
  reference_model_hint: Generate the Python FunctionalModel from function_model transactions and compare timer_count_q, prdata, pready, pslverr, and irq against RTL observations.

cycle_model:
  purpose: Cycle-level APB, countdown, reload, and irq timing contract for RTL and scoreboard generation.
  executable: python
  cosim: true
  state_accumulating: true
  use_per_cycle_expected: true
  clock: pclk
  reset:
    assertion: presetn low asynchronously clears load_q, enable_q, timer_count_q, and irq_q to zero.
    deassertion: On the first rising edge after synchronized reset deassertion, counter remains zero and disabled until programmed.
  latency:
    register_read: { min_cycles: 0, max_cycles: 1, description: APB read data is valid in the access phase when pready is asserted. }
    register_write: { min_cycles: 0, max_cycles: 1, description: APB write is accepted in the access phase when pready is asserted. }
    tick_update: { min_cycles: 1, max_cycles: 1, description: Enabled timer updates once per pclk cycle when no APB access overrides the sampled transaction. }
    irq_pulse: { min_cycles: 1, max_cycles: 1, description: irq is asserted for exactly one clock cycle on expiry. }
  handshake_rules:
    - id: APB_SINGLE_CYCLE_READY
      signal: pready
      rule: pready is 1 during psel==1 and penable==1 access phase, otherwise 0.
      expr: (psel == 1 and penable == 1)
    - id: APB_INVALID_ERROR
      signal: pslverr
      rule: pslverr is 1 only for invalid addresses or writes to read-only STATUS during accepted APB access.
      expr: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and ((paddr != 0 and paddr != 4 and paddr != 8) or (pwrite == 1 and paddr == 8))
    - id: IRQ_SINGLE_CYCLE
      signal: irq
      rule: irq is asserted for one pclk cycle when enable_q==1 and timer_count_q==0, then deasserts unless the next sampled cycle is another expiry.
      expr: enable_q == 1 and timer_count_q == 0
  pipeline:
    - stage: IDLE
      cycle: 0
      action: No APB access; timer tick path may update counter if enabled.
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_idle, port: pready, expr: '0', width: 1 }
        - { name: pslverr_idle, port: pslverr, expr: '0', width: 1 }
    - stage: APB_SETUP
      cycle: 0
      action: psel may assert with penable low; no register transfer is committed.
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_setup, port: pready, expr: '0', width: 1 }
        - { name: pslverr_setup, port: pslverr, expr: '0', width: 1 }
    - stage: APB_ACCESS
      cycle: 1
      action: APB transfer commits when psel and penable are asserted; pready indicates completion.
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_access, port: pready, expr: '1', width: 1 }
        - { name: pslverr_access, port: pslverr, expr: '1 if ((paddr != 0 and paddr != 4 and paddr != 8) or (pwrite == 1 and paddr == 8)) else 0', width: 1 }
    - stage: TICK_COMMIT
      cycle: 1
      action: On each pclk edge with no APB transfer selected, enabled timer decrements or reloads/pulses irq.
      output_rules:
        - { name: irq_tick, port: irq, expr: '1 if (enable_q == 1 and timer_count_q == 0 and psel == 0) else 0', width: 1 }
  ordering:
    - APB writes to LOAD or CTRL commit before subsequent timer tick observations in following cycles.
    - Writing ENABLE=0 stops the counter and causes following timer ticks to hold timer_count_q.
    - Expiry observes timer_count_q==0 while enabled, asserts irq for one cycle, and reloads timer_count_q from load_q on the same clock edge.
    - STATUS reads do not modify timer_count_q, load_q, enable_q, or irq generation behavior.
  backpressure:
    - APB-style interface has no extended backpressure; pready completes every access phase in one cycle.
  observability:
    - Scoreboard samples APB access results at APB_ACCESS and timer tick results at TICK_COMMIT.

clock_reset_domains:
  domains:
    - name: pclk_domain
      frequency_mhz: 100
      description: Single APB/timer clock domain.
  reset_scheme:
    signal: presetn
    polarity: active_low
    type: async_assert_sync_deassert

cdc_requirements:
  crossings: []
  synchronizers: []
  note: Single pclk domain; no clock-domain crossings.

rdc_requirements:
  crossings: []
  synchronizers: []
  note: Single reset domain; no reset-domain crossings.

registers:
  config:
    register_width: 32
    addr_width: 8
    byte_addressable: true
    no_registers: false
  register_list:
    - name: LOAD
      offset: 0x00
      width: 32
      access: rw
      reset: 0x00000000
      category: control
      description: Reload value captured by software; used when the enabled timer expires.
      fields:
        - name: load_value
          bits: [31, 0]
          access: rw
          reset: 0x00000000
          description: 32-bit reload value.
          side_effects:
            write: Updates load_q; does not directly change current STATUS count.
            read: Returns current load_q.
    - name: CTRL
      offset: 0x04
      width: 32
      access: rw
      reset: 0x00000000
      category: control
      description: Timer control register.
      fields:
        - name: enable
          bits: [0, 0]
          access: rw
          reset: 0x0
          description: 1 enables countdown; 0 disables and holds current counter value.
          side_effects:
            write: Updates enable_q from bit 0.
            read: Returns current enable_q.
        - name: reserved
          bits: [31, 1]
          access: ro
          reset: 0x0
          description: Reserved, reads as zero, writes ignored.
    - name: STATUS
      offset: 0x08
      width: 32
      access: ro
      reset: 0x00000000
      category: status
      description: Current timer count value.
      fields:
        - name: count
          bits: [31, 0]
          access: ro
          reset: 0x00000000
          description: Current down-counter value.
          side_effects:
            read: Returns timer_count_q without modifying timer state.
            write: Illegal; APB write to STATUS reports pslverr.

memory:
  instances: []
  note: Timer uses only scalar flops for load, enable, count, and irq pulse; no SRAM or FIFO.

interrupts:
  sources:
    - name: IRQ_PULSE
      bit: 0
      type: pulse
      enable_reg: CTRL.enable
      status_reg: none
      clear: self_clear_one_cycle
      description: Active-high single-cycle pulse when enabled timer reaches zero and reloads LOAD.
  output:
    signal: irq
    polarity: active_high
    type: pulse

fsm:
  timer_control:
    states:
      - RESET_HOLD
      - DISABLED_HOLD
      - COUNTING
      - EXPIRE_RELOAD_PULSE
    transitions:
      - { from: RESET_HOLD, to: DISABLED_HOLD, condition: presetn == 1 and enable_q == 0 }
      - { from: DISABLED_HOLD, to: COUNTING, condition: enable_q == 1 }
      - { from: COUNTING, to: DISABLED_HOLD, condition: enable_q == 0 }
      - { from: COUNTING, to: EXPIRE_RELOAD_PULSE, condition: enable_q == 1 and timer_count_q == 0 }
      - { from: EXPIRE_RELOAD_PULSE, to: COUNTING, condition: enable_q == 1 }
      - { from: EXPIRE_RELOAD_PULSE, to: DISABLED_HOLD, condition: enable_q == 0 }
    reset_state: RESET_HOLD

rtl_contract:
  reset_behavior:
    - presetn low clears LOAD, CTRL.ENABLE, STATUS.count, and irq to zero.
    - After reset deassertion counter is disabled and holds zero until software writes CTRL.ENABLE=1.
  apb_contract:
    - LOAD offset 0x00 is readable/writable.
    - CTRL offset 0x04 bit 0 is readable/writable; bits 31:1 read zero and ignore writes.
    - STATUS offset 0x08 is read-only and exposes current count.
    - Invalid APB addresses and writes to STATUS assert pslverr for the accepted access.
  timer_contract:
    - While enabled and count is non-zero, count decrements by one each pclk cycle.
    - While enabled and count is zero, irq pulses for one cycle and count reloads LOAD.
    - While disabled, count holds its value and irq is deasserted.

coding_rules:
  verilog_style: systemverilog_2012
  file_extension: .sv
  parameter_header: rtl/timer_param.vh
  conventions:
    - Use ANSI-style SystemVerilog ports with logic types.
    - Use nonblocking assignments in sequential always blocks.
    - Use blocking assignments in combinational always blocks.
    - No inferred latches; assign all combinational outputs in every path.
    - Active-low reset named presetn.
    - Keep APB decode and timer core ownership separated according to sub_modules.
  lint_waivers: []

reuse_modules: []

custom:
  assumptions:
    - APB register response is single-cycle with no wait-state insertion.
    - APB byte strobes are not present in the user requirement; full 32-bit writes are assumed for starter SSOT.
    - Loading LOAD does not immediately load STATUS.count; STATUS.count changes only by reset, timer tick, or reload at expiry.
  starter_mode: true


timing:
  target_clocks:
    - name: pclk
      frequency_mhz: 100
      period_ns: 10.0
      uncertainty_ns: 0.2
      description: Timer/APB clock target for starter implementation.
  latency_budget:
    apb_read_cycles: 1
    apb_write_cycles: 1
    tick_update_cycles: 1
    irq_pulse_cycles: 1
  throughput:
    timer_tick: One counter update per pclk cycle while enabled.
    apb_access: One accepted access per APB access phase.

power:
  domains:
    - name: PD_TIMER
      supplies:
        - VDD
        - VSS
      clock: pclk
      description: Single always-on timer power domain.
  power_states:
    - name: ON
      domain: PD_TIMER
      behavior: Registers retain and timer operates normally.
    - name: RESET
      domain: PD_TIMER
      behavior: presetn asserted low clears architectural state.
  clock_gating:
    allowed: true
    condition: enable_q == 0 and psel == 0
    requirement: Clock gating, if implemented, must preserve APB register accessibility and disabled counter hold behavior.

security:
  classification: non_security_critical_control_peripheral
  assets:
    - name: timer_configuration
      description: LOAD and CTRL settings programmed by software.
    - name: interrupt_integrity
      description: irq pulse timing used by firmware scheduling or timeout handling.
  threat_model:
    - threat: Unauthorized or erroneous APB writes can change timer period or enable state.
      mitigation: Integration must place timer in the intended APB address map and rely on upstream bus access control.
    - threat: Invalid register access could hide software bugs.
      mitigation: Invalid addresses and writes to STATUS assert pslverr.
  safety_goals:
    - irq must not stick high; it is a one-cycle pulse per expiry event.
    - Reset must leave timer disabled with count zero.

error_handling:
  error_sources:
    - name: INVALID_APB_ADDRESS
      condition: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and paddr not in {0x00,0x04,0x08}
      architectural_effect: pslverr asserted for access; no architectural timer state update.
    - name: WRITE_READ_ONLY_STATUS
      condition: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and paddr == 0x08
      architectural_effect: pslverr asserted for access; STATUS/count not modified by the write.
  propagation:
    - APB access errors propagate only through pslverr and do not assert irq.
    - Timer expiry propagates through irq pulse and STATUS reload behavior, not through pslverr.
  recovery:
    - Software may retry legal APB accesses after an error with no reset required.
    - Reset clears all state to disabled zero-count state.


debug_observability:
  waveform_must_probe:
    - pclk
    - presetn
    - psel
    - penable
    - pwrite
    - paddr
    - pwdata
    - prdata
    - pready
    - pslverr
    - load_q
    - enable_q
    - timer_count_q
    - irq
  trace_events:
    - id: TRACE_APB_WRITE_LOAD
      trigger: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and paddr == 0
      payload:
        - pwdata
    - id: TRACE_APB_WRITE_CTRL
      trigger: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and paddr == 4
      payload:
        - pwdata
        - enable_q
    - id: TRACE_TIMER_EXPIRE
      trigger: enable_q == 1 and timer_count_q == 0
      payload:
        - load_q
        - irq
    - id: TRACE_APB_ERROR
      trigger: pslverr == 1
      payload:
        - paddr
        - pwrite

integration:
  bus_attachment:
    bus: APB-style
    role: slave
    base_address: integration-assigned
    address_span_bytes: 16
    clock: pclk
    reset: presetn
  dependencies:
    - Upstream APB master supplies stable paddr, pwrite, and pwdata through access phase.
    - irq output is connected to the SoC interrupt aggregation or firmware-visible interrupt input.
    - No external memories or child reusable IPs are required.
  connections:
    - { from_module: timer, from_port: pclk, to_module: timer_regs, to_port: clk, signal: pclk }
    - { from_module: timer, from_port: presetn, to_module: timer_regs, to_port: resetn, signal: presetn }
    - { from_module: timer, from_port: pclk, to_module: timer_core, to_port: clk, signal: pclk }
    - { from_module: timer, from_port: presetn, to_module: timer_core, to_port: resetn, signal: presetn }
    - { from_module: timer_regs, from_port: load_q, to_module: timer_core, to_port: load_i, signal: load_q }
    - { from_module: timer_regs, from_port: enable_q, to_module: timer_core, to_port: enable_i, signal: enable_q }
    - { from_module: timer_core, from_port: count_o, to_module: timer_regs, to_port: count_q, signal: timer_count_q }
    - { from_module: timer_core, from_port: irq_o, to_module: timer, to_port: irq, signal: irq }

dft:
  scan_required: false
  controllability:
    - pclk and presetn are directly controllable at top level.
    - APB inputs can program LOAD and CTRL and exercise STATUS readback.
  observability:
    - irq and APB prdata/pslverr expose functional outcomes.
    - Internal load_q, enable_q, and timer_count_q are required waveform probes for simulation debug.
  mbist_required: false

synthesis:
  dialect: systemverilog_2012
  constraints:
    clock: pclk
    reset: presetn active_low
    target_frequency_mhz: 100
    max_area: null
    max_power_mw: null
  required_outputs:
    - synthesized_netlist
    - timing_report
    - area_report
    - power_report
    - lint_report


dir_structure:
  template_dirs:
    rtl: templates/rtl/
    sim: templates/sim/
  output_dirs:
    rtl: rtl/
    sim: sim/
    tb: tb/
    tc: tc/
    firmware: firmware/
    docs: doc/
  yaml_dir: yaml/
  generators_dir: generators/

filelist:
  headers:
    - rtl/timer_param.vh
  rtl:
    - rtl/timer.sv
    - rtl/timer_regs.sv
    - rtl/timer_core.sv
  sim:
    - sim/tb_top.sv
  firmware:
    - firmware/timer_regs.h
  docs:
    - doc/timer_register_map.md
    - doc/timer_mas.md

test_requirements:
  scenarios:
    - id: SC_RESET_DEFAULTS
      name: Reset leaves disabled zero-count timer
      stimulus: Assert presetn low then deassert and sample outputs/registers.
      stimulus_machine_spec:
        timeline:
          - { cycle: 0, assign: { presetn: 0, psel: 0, penable: 0, pwrite: 0, paddr: 0, pwdata: 0 } }
          - { cycle: 2, assign: { presetn: 1 } }
          - { cycle: 3, csr_write: { register: CTRL, data: 0 } }
      expected: load_q, enable_q, timer_count_q, irq are zero; timer remains disabled.
      checker: FL-vs-RTL scoreboard compares reset state and no irq.
      coverage:
        - FCOV_RESET
        - CCOV_RESET_DEASSERT
    - id: SC_APB_PROGRAM_LOAD_ENABLE
      name: Program LOAD and enable timer
      stimulus: Write LOAD=3 then CTRL.ENABLE=1 using APB-style accesses.
      stimulus_machine_spec:
        csr_writes:
          - { register: LOAD, data: 3 }
          - { register: CTRL, data: 1 }
      expected: load_q becomes 3, enable_q becomes 1, counter begins enabled countdown behavior.
      checker: Structured scoreboard events check APB write acceptance and state mirrors.
      coverage:
        - FCOV_APB_WRITE_LOAD
        - FCOV_APB_WRITE_CTRL
        - CCOV_APB_ACCESS
    - id: SC_COUNTDOWN_IRQ_RELOAD
      name: Enabled countdown generates irq and reloads
      stimulus: Program LOAD=2, enable timer, then wait enough pclk cycles for expiry.
      stimulus_machine_spec:
        timeline:
          - { cycle: 0, csr_write: { register: LOAD, data: 2 } }
          - { cycle: 1, csr_write: { register: CTRL, data: 1 } }
          - { cycle: 2, wait: 5 }
      expected: Counter decrements each cycle while enabled; when count reaches zero, irq pulses for one cycle and count reloads LOAD.
      checker: FL-vs-RTL per-cycle comparison of timer_count_q and irq.
      coverage:
        - FCOV_TICK_DECREMENT
        - FCOV_TICK_EXPIRE_RELOAD
        - CCOV_IRQ_SINGLE_CYCLE
    - id: SC_DISABLE_HOLDS
      name: Disable stops and holds current count
      stimulus: Enable timer, allow at least one decrement, write CTRL.ENABLE=0, then wait.
      stimulus_machine_spec:
        timeline:
          - { cycle: 0, csr_write: { register: LOAD, data: 5 } }
          - { cycle: 1, csr_write: { register: CTRL, data: 1 } }
          - { cycle: 2, wait: 2 }
          - { cycle: 4, csr_write: { register: CTRL, data: 0 } }
          - { cycle: 5, wait: 3 }
      expected: After disable write, STATUS.count holds stable and irq remains low.
      checker: Scoreboard checks no further decrement while enable_q is zero.
      coverage:
        - FCOV_TICK_DISABLED
        - CCOV_DISABLE_HOLD
    - id: SC_APB_READ_STATUS
      name: STATUS read exposes current count
      stimulus: Program timer and perform APB read of STATUS.
      stimulus_machine_spec:
        timeline:
          - { cycle: 0, csr_write: { register: LOAD, data: 4 } }
          - { cycle: 1, csr_write: { register: CTRL, data: 1 } }
          - { cycle: 2, wait: 1 }
          - { cycle: 3, assign: { psel: 1, penable: 1, pwrite: 0, paddr: 8 } }
      expected: prdata equals current timer_count_q and STATUS read has no side effect.
      checker: APB read checker compares prdata against FunctionalModel state.
      coverage:
        - FCOV_APB_READ_STATUS
        - CCOV_APB_ACCESS
    - id: SC_APB_INVALID_ACCESS
      name: Invalid register access reports pslverr
      stimulus: Issue APB access to unmapped address and write to STATUS.
      stimulus_machine_spec:
        timeline:
          - { cycle: 0, assign: { psel: 1, penable: 1, pwrite: 0, paddr: 12, pwdata: 0 } }
          - { cycle: 1, assign: { psel: 1, penable: 1, pwrite: 1, paddr: 8, pwdata: 4294967295 } }
      expected: pslverr asserted for invalid access; timer architectural state is not corrupted.
      checker: Error/status assertion plus FL-vs-RTL state comparison.
      coverage:
        - FCOV_APB_INVALID
        - CCOV_APB_ERROR
  scoreboard_checks: 8
  coverage_goals:
    function:
      target_pct: 100
      model: function_model
      description: Functional coverage for APB programming, countdown, reload, disable hold, status read, and invalid access.
      bins:
        - { id: FCOV_APB_WRITE_LOAD, source_ref: function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_LOAD, class: transaction, description: LOAD write accepted. }
        - { id: FCOV_APB_WRITE_CTRL, source_ref: function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_CTRL, class: transaction, description: CTRL enable write accepted. }
        - { id: FCOV_APB_READ_STATUS, source_ref: function_model.transactions.FM_APB_READ_STATUS, class: transaction, description: STATUS read returns count. }
        - { id: FCOV_APB_INVALID, source_ref: function_model.transactions.FM_APB_INVALID, class: error_transaction, description: Invalid access produces pslverr. }
        - { id: FCOV_TICK_DISABLED, source_ref: function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DISABLED, class: state_hold, description: Disabled timer holds count. }
        - { id: FCOV_TICK_DECREMENT, source_ref: function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DECREMENT, class: state_update, description: Enabled non-zero timer decrements. }
        - { id: FCOV_TICK_EXPIRE_RELOAD, source_ref: function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_EXPIRE_RELOAD, class: state_update, description: Expiry reloads and pulses irq. }
    cycle:
      target_pct: 100
      model: cycle_model
      description: Cycle and handshake coverage for APB response and timer tick behavior.
      bins:
        - { id: CCOV_APB_ACCESS, source_ref: cycle_model.pipeline.APB_ACCESS, class: pipeline_stage, description: APB access phase observed. }
        - { id: CCOV_IRQ_SINGLE_CYCLE, source_ref: cycle_model.handshake_rules.IRQ_SINGLE_CYCLE, class: pulse, description: irq one-cycle pulse observed. }
        - { id: CCOV_APB_ERROR, source_ref: cycle_model.handshake_rules.APB_INVALID_ERROR, class: error_handshake, description: pslverr behavior observed. }
        - { id: CCOV_DISABLE_HOLD, source_ref: cycle_model.ordering, class: ordering, description: Disable hold ordering observed. }
    functional: Function and cycle coverage must close for starter downstream generation.
    code: DUT-only compile and DUT-only lint required; line/branch metrics may be defaulted by repair flow in starter mode.

pnr:
  utilization_pct: 60
  aspect_ratio: 1.0
  core_space_um: 2.0
  global_density: 0.65
  io_layers: { horizontal: met3, vertical: met2 }
  cts: { buf_list: [] }

quality_gates:

workflow_todos:
  ssot-gen:
    - id: SSOT_STARTER_VALIDATE_TIMER
      content: Validate starter SSOT for timer and allow deterministic repair defaults where starter mode permits.
      detail: Run repair_ssot_schema.py and check_ssot_disk.sh in starter mode against timer/yaml/timer.ssot.yaml.
      command: /to-ssot timer
      script: $ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/ssot-gen/scripts/check_ssot_disk.sh
      instructions:
        - Use timer/req/timer_requirements.md as the semantic source.
        - Do not add behavior that contradicts the reset, enable, decrement, reload, irq pulse, or STATUS read requirements.
      criteria:
        - function_model has non-empty state_variables, transactions, and invariants.
        - check_ssot_disk.sh timer --mode starter reports pass after repair.
      source_refs:
        - timer/req/timer_requirements.md
      priority: high
      required: true
  fl-model-gen:
    - id: FL_TIMER_EXECUTABLE_MODEL
      content: Generate executable FunctionalModel for timer countdown and APB register semantics.
      detail: Implement state updates for load_q, enable_q, timer_count_q, and irq_q exactly from function_model transactions.
      command: /ssot-fl-model timer
      script: $ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/fl-model-gen/scripts/generate_fl_model.py
      instructions:
        - Preserve transaction ordering with specific APB/tick transactions before FM_IDLE.
        - Expose expected outputs prdata, pready, pslverr, and irq for scoreboard use.
      criteria:
        - FunctionalModel can execute reset, LOAD write, CTRL write, tick decrement, expiry reload, disable hold, STATUS read, and invalid access.
      source_refs:
        - function_model
      priority: high
      required: true
  rtl-gen:
    - id: RTL_TIMER_TOP_INTEGRATION
      content: Author timer.sv top-level integration from SSOT ports and submodule connections.
      detail: Instantiate timer_regs and timer_core, wire APB ports to timer_regs, wire load_q/enable_q/count_q between regs and core, and expose irq from timer_core.
      command: /gen-rtl timer
      script: $ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/rtl-gen/scripts/derive_rtl_todos.py
      instructions:
        - Use io_list as the top port authority.
        - Use integration.connections and sub_modules[].connections for module wiring.
        - Do not create extra wrapper-only top files; top file is rtl/timer.sv.
      criteria:
        - timer.sv contains top module timer with all declared io_list ports.
        - Internal wiring matches module/port/signal records.
      source_refs:
        - top_module
        - io_list
        - integration.connections
        - sub_modules
      owner_module: timer
      owner_file: rtl/timer.sv
      priority: high
      required: true
    - id: RTL_TIMER_APB_REGISTERS
      content: Implement APB-style register decode for LOAD, CTRL, and STATUS.
      detail: LOAD offset 0x00 captures pwdata[31:0], CTRL offset 0x04 captures enable bit, STATUS offset 0x08 returns current count, pready is asserted in access phase, and invalid accesses assert pslverr without corrupting state.
      command: /gen-rtl timer
      script: $ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/rtl-gen/scripts/derive_rtl_todos.py
      instructions:
        - Implement register resets to zero on presetn low.
        - Reserved CTRL bits read as zero and ignore writes.
        - Writes to STATUS must assert pslverr and not change timer_count_q.
      criteria:
        - APB read/write behavior matches function_model transactions FM_APB_WRITE_LOAD, FM_APB_WRITE_CTRL, FM_APB_READ_STATUS, and FM_APB_INVALID.
        - DUT-only compile and lint include timer_regs.sv.
      source_refs:
        - registers.register_list
        - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_LOAD
        - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_WRITE_CTRL
        - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_READ_STATUS
        - function_model.transactions.FM_APB_INVALID
        - cycle_model.handshake_rules.APB_SINGLE_CYCLE_READY
      owner_module: timer_regs
      owner_file: rtl/timer_regs.sv
      priority: high
      required: true
    - id: RTL_TIMER_COUNTDOWN_CORE
      content: Implement enable-controlled countdown, hold, expiry reload, and single-cycle irq pulse.
      detail: While enable_q is zero hold count and keep irq low; while enable_q is one and count is non-zero decrement by one each pclk; when enable_q is one and count is zero pulse irq for one cycle and reload count from load_q.
      command: /gen-rtl timer
      script: $ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/rtl-gen/scripts/derive_rtl_todos.py
      instructions:
        - Reset count and irq to zero on presetn low.
        - Ensure irq is a pulse, not a sticky level.
        - Ensure reload uses the current load_q value.
      criteria:
        - RTL observed timer_count_q and irq match FM_TICK_DISABLED, FM_TICK_DECREMENT, and FM_TICK_EXPIRE_RELOAD under FL-vs-RTL comparison.
        - Countdown behavior is covered by SC_COUNTDOWN_IRQ_RELOAD and SC_DISABLE_HOLDS.
      source_refs:
        - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DISABLED
        - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_DECREMENT
        - function_model.transactions.FM_TICK_EXPIRE_RELOAD
        - cycle_model.pipeline.TICK_COMMIT
        - interrupts.sources.IRQ_PULSE
      owner_module: timer_core
      owner_file: rtl/timer_core.sv
      priority: high
      required: true
    - id: RTL_TIMER_RESET_AND_IRQ_ASSERTIONS
      content: Add implementation-visible reset and irq pulse behavior suitable for downstream checks.
      detail: RTL must make reset state and one-cycle irq pulse observable through ports and internal probes named in debug_observability.
      command: /gen-rtl timer
      script: $ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/rtl-gen/scripts/derive_rtl_todos.py
      instructions:
        - Preserve signal names or trace aliases for load_q, enable_q, timer_count_q, and irq.
        - Avoid optimizations that obscure required simulation debug probes in starter flow.
      criteria:
        - SC_RESET_DEFAULTS and SC_COUNTDOWN_IRQ_RELOAD can produce structured scoreboard events with matching FL and RTL values.
      source_refs:
        - debug_observability.waveform_must_probe
        - function_model.invariants
        - cycle_model.handshake_rules.IRQ_SINGLE_CYCLE
      owner_module: timer_core
      owner_file: rtl/timer_core.sv
      priority: medium
      required: true
  tb-gen:
    - id: TB_TIMER_SCOREBOARD_SCENARIOS
      content: Generate cocotb/pyuvm tests and structured scoreboard events for timer scenarios.
      detail: Drive APB-style register writes/reads and per-cycle waits according to test_requirements.scenarios[].stimulus_machine_spec.
      command: /ssot-tb timer
      script: $ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/tb-gen/scripts/generate_tb.py
      instructions:
        - Compare prdata, pready, pslverr, irq, and exposed count/debug state against FunctionalModel.
        - Emit structured scoreboard events for each scenario.
      criteria:
        - All declared scenarios run and produce FL-vs-RTL comparison evidence.
      source_refs:
        - test_requirements.scenarios
        - function_model
        - cycle_model
      priority: high
      required: true
  sim_debug: []
  coverage: []
  syn: []
  pnr: []
  sta: []
  sta-post: []

generation_flow:
  steps:
    - name: repair_ssot
      command: python3 "$ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/ssot-gen/scripts/repair_ssot_schema.py" timer --root "$ATLAS_PROJECT_ROOT" --mode starter
      description: Fill deterministic starter boilerplate defaults without changing timer semantics.
    - name: verify_ssot
      command: bash "$ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/ssot-gen/scripts/check_ssot_disk.sh" timer --root "$ATLAS_PROJECT_ROOT" --mode starter
      description: Validate starter SSOT before downstream generation.
    - name: handoff_fl_model
      command: /ssot-fl-model timer
      description: Generate executable FunctionalModel and equivalence goals.
    - name: handoff_rtl
      command: /gen-rtl timer
      description: Author RTL from SSOT-owned timer contract.
    - name: handoff_tb
      command: /ssot-tb timer
      description: Generate cocotb/pyuvm tests and structured scoreboard.
